title: Real Estate Social Media Schedulers in 2026: MLS Auto-Post vs Multi-Platform Planners
description: Hootsuite, Buffer, SocialBee, Loomly, Agorapulse—pricing, trade-offs, and which scheduler fits real estate content workflows in 2026.
tldr: Real estate agents need schedulers that handle multiple platforms, bulk uploads, and team workflows—not just MLS auto-posts. Buffer and SocialBee offer the lowest entry points ($5/mo and $24/mo respectively). Hootsuite, Loomly, and Agorapulse bring approval workflows and deeper analytics for teams managing multiple agents or client accounts.

body.text: Real estate social posting splits into two camps: agents auto-syncing MLS listings to Facebook, and agents running multi-platform content calendars with open houses, market updates, and community posts. Pure MLS auto-posters are niche IDX or CRM add-ons—this review covers general-purpose schedulers that handle Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, TikTok, YouTube, and Pinterest. The trade-off: you gain control, analytics, and team workflows; you lose one-click MLS syndication. Pricing ranges from $5/mo for solo agents to $249/mo for agencies managing 60+ profiles.

We pulled pricing and feature lists from each vendor's public pages in May 2026. We did not test these tools ourselves or run agent accounts through trial periods. Conclusions reflect published capabilities, not measured conversion rates or posting speeds. If a feature isn't listed in the research bundle, we note its absence or omit the claim.

body.name: Buffer
body.price: Free (3 channels, 10 posts/channel refill), $5/mo Essentials (1 channel, unlimited posts), $10/mo Team (1 channel, unlimited team members)—billed yearly saves 2 months
pros: Lowest barrier to entry—free tier covers 3 profiles with 10-post refill per channel
Essentials at $5/mo/channel includes AI Assistant, advanced analytics, hashtag manager
Team plan adds unlimited members, approval workflows, access levels—$10/mo/channel yearly
Supports Bluesky, Threads, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, Pinterest, X, Google Business Profile, Mastodon
cons: Per-channel pricing scales fast—3 profiles on Essentials = $15/mo; 10 profiles = $50/mo
No bulk CSV upload or post-tagging visible in feature list
Analytics described as 'advanced' but no competitor benchmarking or sentiment tracking

body.text: Buffer's per-channel model punishes agents juggling multiple Instagram business accounts or separate Facebook pages for listings vs personal brand. A solo agent posting to Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, and Google Business needs 4 channels: $20/mo on Essentials yearly, $40/mo monthly. Team plan makes sense if you're splitting content approval between an assistant and a photographer—unlimited users for $10/mo/channel is cheaper than seat-based competitors once you hit 3+ collaborators per profile.

body.name: SocialBee
body.price: $24/mo Bootstrap (5 profiles, 1 user, yearly), $40/mo Accelerate (10 profiles, 1 user), $82/mo Pro (25 profiles, 3 users, 5 workspaces)—monthly plans $29/$49/$99; agency plans start $149/mo for 50 profiles
pros: Profile-bundled pricing—5 profiles for $24/mo yearly vs Buffer's per-channel $25/mo
Content categories and recurring post queues suit repetitive listing/open-house cycles
CSV bulk upload, hashtag organizer, first-comment scheduling on Bootstrap+
Pro plan includes unlimited AI generation, post approval, internal notes, 2-year analytics retention
Supports Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X, Pinterest, TikTok, YouTube, Google Business, Bluesky, Threads, Universal Posting
cons: Bootstrap limited to 10 content categories and 10 content sources—restrictive for agents cycling neighborhood/listing/market-update queues
No multi-user access until $82/mo Pro—solo agents can't delegate to an assistant without upgrading
Agency pricing jumps to $149/mo for 50 profiles (vs Loomly's 12 profiles at $49/mo yearly)

body.text: SocialBee's content-category system aligns with real estate posting rhythms: one queue for Monday market updates, another for Thursday open houses, a third for Friday neighborhood spotlights. Agents who batch-create posts love the CSV upload and recurring schedules. Limitation: you can't add a VA or transaction coordinator until you jump to Pro at $82/mo, while Buffer Team gives unlimited users at $10/channel.

body.name: Loomly
body.price: $49/mo Starter (12 profiles, 3 users, yearly; $65/mo monthly), $249/mo Beyond (60 profiles, unlimited users), Custom pricing for Enterprise (61+ profiles, priority support)
pros: Starter includes 12 profiles for $49/mo yearly—covers Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, X, Pinterest, TikTok, Google Business, YouTube, Threads
Approval workflows, custom roles, hashtag manager, 2FA enforcement at Starter tier
Post inspiration 'Ideas board' and media editor built-in—no Canva/Adobe dependency
Bulk posting, best-time-to-post, link shortener analytics, Slack/Teams integration on Starter
Beyond plan scales to 60 profiles with unlimited users, custom branding, extended AI usage
cons: No free tier or per-channel option—minimum $49/mo yearly commitment
Starter limits AI social listening to 1/month, reply-with-AI restricted—less useful for reputation monitoring
Feature list omits CSV import or bulk upload confirmation

body.text: Loomly's 12-profile bundle at $49/mo yearly beats Buffer's 12-channel $60/mo and SocialBee's 10-profile $40/mo once you factor in the included 3 users and approval workflows. Real estate teams—broker + listing agent + marketing coordinator—can collaborate without seat charges. The Ideas board surfaces post prompts; useful if you're stuck on captions. The catch: no monthly flexibility below $65/mo, and AI listening caps at 1 query/month on Starter.

body.name: Hootsuite
body.price: Pricing unlisted per-user/mo; Standard includes 10 profiles, 1+ users; Advanced includes unlimited profiles, 1+ users; Enterprise custom—contact for quote. Published material references annual billing discounts and 30-day free trials.
pros: Advanced plan: unlimited profiles, bulk schedule 350 posts, customizable reports, benchmark 20 competitors
AI assistant (OwlyGPT) generates posts, campaign strategies, hashtags in brand voice
Saved replies, auto-route/tag messages, DM automation, search 30-day mentions (Advanced)
Enterprise adds Amplify employee advocacy, advanced inbox, generative AI chatbot, review management, Salesforce + compliance integrations
cons: No public per-user pricing—requires contact or trial signup to see actual cost
Standard tier caps at 10 profiles; real estate teams hit that fast
Feature density intimidates solo agents—built for corporate social teams, not one-person shops

body.text: Hootsuite's opaque pricing and enterprise feature set (employee advocacy, compliance integration, Salesforce sync) signal corporate buyer focus. The 350-post bulk scheduler and 20-competitor benchmarking appeal to brokerages managing 20+ agent profiles or agencies running client accounts. Solo agents drown in nav complexity. If you need DM automation and competitor sentiment tracking, Hootsuite delivers—but you'll pay more than Buffer or SocialBee for comparable publishing alone.

body.name: Agorapulse
body.price: $79/mo Standard (10 profiles, 1 user, yearly; $99/mo monthly), $119/mo Professional (10 profiles, 1 user), $149/mo Advanced (10 profiles, 1 user), Custom for unlimited profiles—yearly saves up to 20%
pros: Unified inbox handles organic comments, ad comments, DMs across Facebook, Instagram, X, LinkedIn, Pinterest, TikTok, YouTube, Threads, Bluesky, Google Business
Professional adds link-in-bio, Instagram product tagging, calendar notes, ad-comment monitoring, team performance reports
Advanced includes labels, saved replies, automated moderation, bulk actions, competitor benchmarking, ROI analysis, ads reports
Custom plan: unlimited profiles, AI reply suggestions, SSO, multi-step approvals, sentiment analysis, API integrations, dedicated CSM
cons: Standard at $79/mo yearly is pricier than Loomly's 12-profile $49/mo or Buffer's 10-channel $50/mo Essentials
All paid tiers lock at 10 profiles—agents juggling 12+ pages need Custom pricing
No free tier; 30-day trial requires upfront commitment

body.text: Agorapulse shines in engagement management—its unified inbox aggregates ad comments, a feature missing from Buffer and SocialBee's base tiers. Real estate agents running lead-gen Facebook ads need quick DM responses; Agorapulse's saved replies and automated moderation cut response time. The $79/mo entry is steep for solo agents, but teams running ads + organic content across 10 profiles get more value than Hootsuite's black-box pricing. Advanced tier's ROI analysis and competitor benchmarking justify the $149/mo for brokerages measuring cost-per-lead across agent accounts.

items: Solo agent, tight budget, 3-5 profiles: Buffer Free or Essentials ($5/mo/channel yearly). Upgrade to Team when you add an assistant.
Solo agent batch-posting to 5-10 profiles, recurring content cycles: SocialBee Bootstrap ($24/mo yearly). Content categories and CSV upload beat Buffer's per-channel math.
Small team (2-3 people), 10-12 profiles, need approvals: Loomly Starter ($49/mo yearly). 3 included users + approval workflows cheaper than Buffer Team's 10-channel $100/mo.
Brokerage or agency, 20+ profiles, ad-comment monitoring: Agorapulse Advanced ($149/mo) or Custom. Unified inbox + ad-comment support + ROI analysis justify premium over Buffer/SocialBee at scale.
Enterprise brokerage, 60+ profiles, Salesforce/compliance needs: Hootsuite Enterprise (custom pricing). Employee advocacy, multi-step approvals, compliance integration absent in competitors.
Buffer for teams over 10 profiles—per-channel pricing ($10/mo × 15 channels = $150/mo) exceeds Loomly Beyond's 60-profile $249/mo once you factor unlimited users.
SocialBee Bootstrap if you need multi-user access—$82/mo jump to Pro for 3 users vs Loomly's 3 users at $49/mo.
Hootsuite for solo agents—feature density, opaque pricing, and enterprise positioning waste time; Buffer or SocialBee deliver faster ROI.
Agorapulse Standard if you're not running ads—$79/mo for 10 profiles without ad-comment monitoring is expensive vs Loomly's $49/mo for 12 profiles + approvals.
